RETIREMENT PLANNING

Retirement Planning That Actually Works

You've spent decades saving for retirement. Now it's time to turn those savings into a plan that delivers reliable income, minimizes taxes, and ensures your money lasts as long as you do.

Get Your Retirement Plan Learn More
650+ Families Served
$500K+
Minimum for our comprehensive retirement planning services
5 Pillars
Cash, Income, Growth, Taxes, Legacy — all coordinated
30+ Years
Average retirement length our plans must sustain
CHAPTER I: THE RETIREMENT REALITY

The Complexity Challenge

You've accumulated $500K, $1M, or more — but you don't have a clear strategy for when to retire, how much to withdraw, or how to coordinate all your income sources. Most retirees face four critical gaps:

  • Savings without a withdrawal strategy
  • Fear of running out of money
  • Taxes eating into retirement income
  • No coordination between advisors and accounts
"You can't just wing it in retirement. The wrong Social Security decision alone can cost you $100,000 or more over your lifetime."
CHAPTER II: THE RETIREMENT MONEY MAP

Comprehensive Planning for Every Piece of Your Retirement

We don't just manage your investments. We build a comprehensive Retirement Money Map that coordinates income, taxes, Social Security, Medicare, estate planning, and legacy goals — all working together to maximize your retirement security.

Our 5-pillar framework (Cash, Income, Growth, Taxes, Legacy) ensures nothing falls through the cracks. You'll know exactly how much income you'll have, where it's coming from, what taxes you'll owe, and how long your money will last.

  • Retirement income planning and withdrawal strategies
  • Social Security optimization and claiming strategies
  • Tax-efficient distribution planning (Roth conversions, asset location)
  • Medicare planning and IRMAA avoidance
  • Investment management and portfolio allocation
  • Estate planning coordination and legacy strategies
  • Healthcare cost planning and long-term care analysis
  • Ongoing monitoring and adjustment as your needs change
CHAPTER III: OUR PROCESS

Our 3-Step Retirement Planning Process

01

Organize

Gather and organize all your financial information — accounts, income sources, expenses, goals, and concerns. We create a complete picture of your current situation.

02

Plan

Build your customized Retirement Money Map with detailed income projections, tax strategies, Social Security timing, and contingency planning for market volatility and healthcare costs.

03

Implement

Execute the plan with ongoing portfolio management, tax coordination with your CPA, and regular reviews to adjust as tax laws, markets, and your life circumstances change.

COMMON QUESTIONS

Retirement Planning FAQ

Get answers to the most common retirement planning questions.

Schedule Your Visit
It depends on your lifestyle, healthcare needs, and retirement goals. A common rule of thumb is 25x your annual expenses, but this varies widely. We analyze your specific situation — including Social Security, pensions, healthcare costs, and desired lifestyle — to determine your personalized retirement number.
The best time to start is 5-10 years before retirement, but it's never too late. Planning ahead gives you time to optimize Social Security, execute multi-year Roth conversion strategies, and adjust your savings or spending if needed. Even if you're already retired, proper planning can significantly improve your tax efficiency and income longevity.
A retirement planner helps you transition from saving to spending by creating a comprehensive income strategy. This includes coordinating Social Security, pensions, investment withdrawals, tax planning, Medicare decisions, and estate planning. The goal is to maximize your retirement security while minimizing taxes and ensuring your money lasts throughout retirement.
We're fiduciaries who specialize exclusively in retirement planning for families with $500K+ in assets. Unlike large brokerages or insurance agents, we don't earn commissions on product sales. We provide comprehensive planning that coordinates all aspects of retirement — income, taxes, Social Security, Medicare, and estate planning — not just investment management.
Possibly, but it requires careful planning. Early retirement means no Social Security until 62+, no Medicare until 65, and a longer retirement to fund. We model your specific scenario to determine if early retirement is feasible, what income sources you'd use to bridge the gap, how to manage healthcare costs, and what adjustments might be needed to make it work.

Start Building Your Retirement Plan

You've worked hard to accumulate your savings. Now let's work together to turn them into a plan that delivers the retirement you deserve.
